title: juglandales
intro: Juglandales is an order of flowering plants, comprising families that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. This order includes a diverse range of plant families such as the walnut family, Juglandaceae, as well as other less well-known families like the mulberry family, Moraceae, and the cashew family, Anacardiaceae. However, the examples you provided, orchidaceae, euphorbiaceae, and cyperaceae, actually belong to different orders: asparagales (orchidaceae), sapindales (euphorbiaceae), and poales (cyperaceae) respectively.

families.family_desc: Juglandaceae is a family of plants commonly known as walnut or hickory trees, which are native to the Northern Hemisphere and are characterized by their distinctive pinnate leaves and edible nuts.
